repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body: Extmark has support for horizontal scrolling and truncating, but not wrapping. This PR extends `virt_lines_overflow` flags to support "wrap" and "auto" based on proposed changes in #18282 and changes merged through in #32497. Visual of the change: There are areas for optimization such as caching the row counting in `virt_line_rows`, but didn't want to add complexity in this first revision. Close #18282

comment_data.body:
`decor_virt_lines()` now returns the wrapped row count, not the entry count.

- please add a `@returns` doc

`virt_line_rows()` writes `*skip_cells` even when called for non-target virt-lines.

suggestion: Pass NULL for `skip_cells` on non-target iterations (e.g., compute row counts in a first pass, then call once for the target with skip_cells).

I modeled these as flags to stay consistent with the existing design which had `// kVLWrap = 4` as a placeholder, but `virt_lines_overflow` is mutually exclusive according to `api.txt`. I can change the enum to make this more clear if preferred.
Will update the formatting issues throughout the PR in next revision.
Given the location I think `decor_virt_line_rows` makes sense here.

comment_data.body: Why not name this `virt_line_start_row`? Otherwise it's a bit vague.
I think `VirtLineOverflow` should be stored as two bits inside `flags` (like how `MOD_MASK_MULTI_CLICK` flags work), otherwise this seems a bit of waste of memory.
Will change it.

Changed from using flags per comments here: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/40113#discussion_r3355623727 . `VirtLineOverflow`  members are mutually exclusive so representing them as flags seems to not clearly convey their intent.  The reason I placed it on `virt_line` is because its applied per line and allows for easier direct access when referencing the line.

In terms of memory, the waste would be on 32 bit where it takes up 4 extra bytes, but on 64 bit its net 0 due to alignment rounding.
Updated in most recent push.
Change for this is reflected in most recent updates.
Hmm let's wait some time to decide about this.
Thoughts on keeping the overflow field as is for now and tracking the flags option as a follow up?
This test's description doesn't seems to match its content. It doesn't have a 2-cell char at the boundary.
